title: Golden Beetroot
description: Makes beetroot slightly more useful by adding Golden Beetroot, a mildly debuffed Golden Apple.
body:
Beetroot are useless. Nobody farms beetroot. So why not add a use to them? Beetroot can now be used to make Golden Beetroot, a slightly weaker equivalent to the Golden Apple.

The Golden Beetroot is, as stated, a slightly weaker equivalent to the Golden Apple. Instead of 2 minutes of absorption, the Golden Beetroot only gives one, and the potency of the Regeneration is halved. It is crafted in the same way as the Golden Apple, by surrounding a beetroot with gold in a crafting table:
![The Golden Beetroot crafting recipe, showing a beetroot in a crafting table surrounded by gold](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/4fb022c705c5230a0ef5c9a876a0bcbe9d3a81a4.png)

I made this because I found beetroot to be a very underused item due to their lack of useful features, and this was the best idea I could think of. I mean, it looks *kind of* similar to an apple, right?

Please note that there is a resource pack bundled in, but it's not a *requirement,* it's only there if you want your golden beetroot to, yknow, look like a beetroot. It's still usable without it, just a nice aesthetic touch.
